name: A Prophet Like Unto Moses
notes: Moses promised that the LORD would raise up a Prophet from among the brethren of Israel, like unto Moses himself, whom the people must hear. The apostles proclaim this promise fulfilled in Christ: Peter cites it to the people in Solomon's porch, and Stephen rehearses it before the council, both quoting Moses nearly word for word. The shared core is the raising up of a Prophet of their brethren, like unto Moses, whom every soul is bound to hear. Deuteronomy gives the original word both in Moses' address (18:15) and in the LORD's own speech to Moses (18:18-19), where the warning is that the LORD will require it of whoever will not hearken. Peter draws that warning forward into Acts 3:23, declaring that the soul which will not hear that prophet shall be destroyed from among the people. Stephen's citation (7:37) keeps the promise itself without the attached warning, fixing his witness on the identity of the coming Prophet.
